annotations.label: Cook–Levin theorem (SAT is NP-complete)

anchor.snippet: states that the Boolean satisfiability problem is NP-complete

annotations.note: Mathlib has Turing-machine computability but defines no complexity classes (P, NP), no SAT, and no NP-completeness, so Cook–Levin is absent.

annotations.label: Polynomial SAT algorithm implies P = NP

anchor.snippet: if there exists a deterministic polynomial-time algorithm for solving Boolean satisfiability

annotations.note: Neither the classes P and NP nor the SAT problem are defined in Mathlib, so this implication is not formalized.

annotations.label: Baker–Gill–Solovay oracle separation

anchor.snippet: there exists an oracle A such that, for all subexponential deterministic-time complexity classes T

annotations.note: Mathlib has relative computability/Turing degrees but no time-bounded complexity classes or relativized P/NP, so this oracle separation is absent.

annotations.label: Levin's optimal-time algorithms

anchor.snippet: he found for each of these problems an algorithm that solves it in optimal time

annotations.note: No notion of optimal-time search algorithms for NP problems exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Decision problem in NP

anchor.snippet: A decision problem is in NP if it can be decided by a nondeterministic Turing machine in polynomial time

annotations.note: Mathlib has deterministic Turing machines but no nondeterministic Turing machines and no polynomial-time class, so the class NP is not defined.

annotations.label: Instance of Boolean satisfiability problem

anchor.snippet: An instance of the Boolean satisfiability problem is a Boolean expression that combines Boolean variables using Boolean operators

annotations.note: Mathlib has Boolean algebras but no propositional/Boolean-formula datatype underlying the SAT problem.

annotations.label: Satisfiable expression

anchor.snippet: Such an expression is satisfiable if there is some assignment of truth values to the variables

annotations.note: Mathlib's `ModelTheory/Satisfiability.lean` covers first-order satisfiability, not propositional truth-assignment satisfiability of Boolean expressions.

annotations.label: SAT is in NP

anchor.snippet: SAT is in NP because any assignment of Boolean values to Boolean variables that is claimed to satisfy the given expression can be verified in polynomial time

annotations.note: Neither SAT nor the class NP is defined in Mathlib, so membership SAT ∈ NP is not formalized.

annotations.label: Every NP problem reduces to SAT

anchor.snippet: every NP problem can be reduced to an instance of a SAT problem by a polynomial-time many-one reduction

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks NP and SAT, and only has computable (not polynomial-time) reductions, so this universal reduction is absent.

annotations.label: Construction of Boolean expression satisfiable iff machine accepts
annotations.note: Mathlib has no Boolean-formula encoding of Turing-machine computations (the tableau construction), so this is not formalized.

annotations.label: Transformation is a polynomial-time reduction

anchor.snippet: Thus the transformation is certainly a polynomial-time many-one reduction

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes computable many-one reductions (`ManyOneReducible`), a resource-unbounded generalization, but not the polynomial-time-bounded reductions needed here.

annotations.label: QBF is PSPACE-complete

anchor.snippet: proving that there exists a problem (the recognition of true quantified Boolean formulas) that is PSPACE-complete

annotations.note: Mathlib defines neither PSPACE nor quantified Boolean formulas, so this completeness result is absent.

annotations.label: DQBF is NL-complete

anchor.snippet: proving that there exists a problem that is NL-complete

annotations.note: Mathlib has no logarithmic-space complexity class NL or associated completeness theory.

annotations.label: NP reduces to SAT in logarithmic space

anchor.snippet: every problem in NP can be reduced in polynomial time (in fact, logarithmic space suffices) to an instance of the Boolean satisfiability problem

annotations.note: Mathlib has neither NP/SAT nor logarithmic-space-bounded reductions, so this refinement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Karp's 21 NP-complete problems

anchor.snippet: 21 diverse combinatorial and graph theoretical problems , each infamous for its intractability, are NP-complete

annotations.note: No NP-completeness framework or any of Karp's 21 problems is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 3SAT is NP-complete

anchor.snippet: he showed the problem 3SAT (the Boolean satisfiability problem for expressions in conjunctive normal form (CNF) with exactly three variables or negations of variables per clause) to be NP-complete

annotations.note: Mathlib has no CNF/3SAT formula representation or NP-completeness, so this result is absent.
